original_hypothesis.title: taxCut Set to Zero Causes Division-by-Zero Bricking All Protocol ETH Flows
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: NukeFund.receive() calculates `devShare = msg.value / taxCut`. EntityForging.forgeWithListed calculates `devFee = forgingFee / taxCut`. EntityTrading.buyNFT calculates `nukeFundContribution = msg.value / taxCut`. If any owner sets taxCut to 0 via the unrestricted setTaxCut function, all three contracts experience division-by-zero reverts, halting minting, trading, and forging.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: setTaxCut has no lower bound validation. A single owner misconfiguration across any of the three contracts bricks its ETH distribution. Since minting sends all ETH to NukeFund, a taxCut=0 on NukeFund halts the entire protocol.

proof_of_signal.pattern: devShare = msg.value / taxCut with no require(taxCut > 0) in setTaxCut. Same pattern in EntityForging.sol:109 and EntityTrading.sol:63

preconditions: Owner calls setTaxCut(0) on NukeFund, EntityForging, or EntityTrading
Any ETH-receiving function is subsequently called

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: msg.value / taxCut where taxCut can be 0
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Owner misconfiguration or malicious action sets taxCut=0
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Complete protocol halt - no new mints, trades, or forges possible

economic_questions: Can the owner recover by setting taxCut back to non-zero?
What is the blast radius of taxCut=0 on each contract?
Are there pending transactions that would revert?
validation_requirements: Call setTaxCut(0) and verify subsequent operations revert
Check if taxCut=1 causes 100% dev share (0% to fund)

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: taxCut default=10, setter has no lower bound
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify division by zero revert on taxCut=0

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
